trend: Bottoming recovery after multi-quarter earnings trough; early signs of margin expansion and deal win momentum
thesis: Tech Mahindra is executing a turnaround under CEO Mohit Joshi with focused cost rationalisation and portfolio pruning that is beginning to reflect in improving EBIT margins quarter-on-quarter. The stock trades at a meaningful discount to Infosys and Wipro on forward P/E, offering catch-up potential as deal wins in telecom and manufacturing verticals accelerate. A rebound in BFSI and communications spend, combined with rupee tailwinds, could drive a re-rating toward large-cap IT peer multiples over the next 1-2 quarters.
risk: Revenue growth recovery remains fragile and any miss on Q1/Q2 FY26 deal ramp-up timelines could extend the de-rating cycle. Elevated exposure to telecom clients (20%+ revenue) makes the stock vulnerable to capex cuts by global telcos amid macro uncertainty.
